definition: The act of directing the mind toward something; attention as an operative mental factor.
notes: The project prefers attention because it is direct and contemporary, and because the term should not be collapsed into sati. Attending is acceptable where the English sentence benefits from a more active verbal-noun feel. The term should be heard as an operative mental directing-toward, which is why the wise and unwise forms matter so much in practice.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and practical contexts
In explanatory prose emphasizing the active movement of the mind toward an object
When the passage is analyzing the mental factor that can be directed wisely or unwisely
context_rules.notes: Keep the operative function of the factor explicit.

example_phrases.notes: Shows the practical verbal sense behind the noun.
Basic doctrinal usage of the term as a mental factor.
